ogTitle: Wallabadah vs Wallabi Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wallabadah and Wallabi Point.
heading: Wallabadah vs Wallabi Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wallabadah, New South Wales and Wallabi Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wallabi Point has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Wallabadah (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wallabadah or Wallabi Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wallabadah has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Wallabi Point scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wallabadah scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Wallabi Point scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wallabadah or Wallabi Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wallabi Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
